mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already at a low level, discount rates on equity cash flows decline and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by positive 20-day SPX performance more often than not.
failure_condition: If triggered on falling FEDFUNDS regimes, SPX delivers negative 20d returns in more than 55% of cases or the average 20d return is not above zero, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration after a low inflation base implies renewed price pressure and tighter expected policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation rises from an already benign level.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers do not coincide with below-zero 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if the conditional mean return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration that will feed into weaker household income growth and lower earnings expectations, pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter. The signal is not present when unemployment is flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rise triggers with PAYEMS slowdown do not produce negative 60d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or the interaction adds no predictive lift over either series alone, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals accelerating domestic demand and housing-linked spending, which supports revenue growth for cyclicals and broad equities over the next month. The effect fails if retail sales rise without housing confirmation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth is positive and HOUST is rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS and rising HOUST do not yield positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered observations or if the average return is not above unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO with stable or declining unemployment indicates improving real output without a labor-market shock, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is weakened if industrial production gains are accompanied by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive while UNRATE is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum without UNRATE deterioration does not produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if adding UNRATE does not improve signal accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 over a quarter signals weakening aggregate demand and lower future revenue growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. The signal is strongest when the GDP contraction is broad rather than transitory.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth periods are not followed by below-zero 20d SPX returns more often than baseline or if the conditional mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, policy tightness and inflation pressure jointly increase discount rates and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week to month. The effect should disappear once inflation falls and policy is no longer restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate plus elevated-inflation episodes do not lead to negative 5d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if returns are not meaningfully worse than when only one of the two conditions is present.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE combined with strong PAYEMS growth signals resilient labor income and consumption support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal fails when payroll gains are concentrated in a single month and unemployment is not improving.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays at or below 4.0% while PAYEMS rises for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment, rising-payroll regimes fail to deliver positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances or if the conditional return is not above unconditional average.
